industries.name: Fundamental Nuclear & Atomic Research
uses: Named after Alfred Nobel. The naming was controversial — a 1957 Nobel Institute claim was quickly disproven, and both Berkeley and Dubna disputed priority for years before IUPAC awarded credit to Dubna in 1997. Nobelium's unusual +2 oxidation state stability (rather than the +3 typical of lanthanides and actinides) reflects relativistic stabilization of the 7s² electron pair.
Longest-lived practical isotope: 259No, t½ = 58 minutes. First synthesized: 1966, JINR Dubna (Flerov group) — confirmed after disputed earlier claims from Stockholm and Berkeley.
Only a few atoms per experiment.

All research conducted at specialized facilities: Oak Ridge National Laboratory (USA), Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (USA), JINR Dubna (Russia), GSI/FAIR (Germany), RIKEN (Japan), and JAEA (Japan).
Study of these elements advances understanding of relativistic quantum chemistry, nuclear shell structure near the heaviest stable nuclei, and the fundamental limits of atomic matter.

companies.name: JINR Dubna
companies.use: Flerov's group at Dubna produced confirmed nobelium in 1966 using curium-242 + carbon-12 fusion and established the nuclear decay properties that definitively identified element 102. IUPAC awarded Dubna priority in 1997. Dubna continues No chemistry studies using the FLNR facilities.

companies.name: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
companies.use: Ghiorso's group at Berkeley independently produced and characterized nobelium, performing atomic beam spectroscopy and aqueous chemistry studies that established No's unique +2 oxidation state preference — a key insight into relativistic effects on heavy actinide chemistry.

companies.name: GSI Helmholtz Centre
companies.use: Performed nuclear spectroscopy of nobelium isotopes using the SHIP separator and FOCAL detector array, measuring detailed gamma-ray and conversion electron spectra that mapped the nuclear energy levels of No-252, No-253, and No-254 — foundational data for understanding the nuclear structure of the heaviest actinides.
